each of those lines is a model that tracks the probable progress of the hurricane’s eye. Any of these could be right and all of them could be wrong. What experts look for is agreement between many models to find the most likely outcome. Hope that helps!

we have to seriously think about attending meteorology school just to understand . Ive learned so much since the 90s following Denis Phillips. I don't even know what color is what , its blurry on these posts. Be safe

There are different groups of scientists who make an estimate - each line is one group's estimate. At this point the best estimate is approximately in the middle of all the lines. That's the best information we have so far.

look for the darkest line in the group (black, dark blue, etc.). That is the consensus of what the “experts” say the path is… this is showing a movement to the south but it is still early to make assumptions. Regardless be prepared and don’t take any risks

I believe they tend to look at the consensus of the models (and I presume experts give weight to the ones they believe more accurate). Currently the consensus appears to be shifting the path slightly south.
